description:
Discovered while investigating fz-kf2. The fz crate depends on fz-runtime
via `path = \"runtime\"`. The runtime crate's Cargo.toml declares both
crate types:

  [lib]
  crate-type = [\"staticlib\", \"rlib\"]

When fz src code changes, cargo rebuilds the rlib correctly. But the
staticlib (.a) is on a separate build path; cargo does NOT always
re-emit the .a in lockstep with the rlib. The fz CLI's `fz build`
invokes `cc ... -L target/debug -lfz_runtime` against the .a — so if
the .a is older than the rlib, the AOT binary links against stale
runtime code while everything else (JIT, interp, fixture matrix's
own driver) uses the rlib.

## Concrete failure mode (observed for fz-kf2)

Layout drift between staticlib and rlib. The .a had an older Process
struct layout where halt_cont_singletons was at a different offset
than the current source. fz_aot_setup wrote at the new offset (because
the C-main emit_aot_c_main used the current rlib's view), fz_aot_run_main
read at the old offset → garbage halt_cl → SIGBUS at the
return_call_indirect inside k_2_s4 (cont seam).

After `cargo clean && cargo build && cargo test`, the 4 AOT SIGBUS
fixture failures vanish — no source change required.

## Repro

Edit any runtime/src/*.rs file's struct layout (e.g., reorder a Process
field), `cargo test --workspace`, then run any AOT fixture. The .a
may be stale.

## Possible fixes

(α) `fz build` invokes `cargo build -p fz-runtime` before linking. Slow
    in the common case but always correct.

(β) Drop the staticlib crate-type, generate the .a on demand from the
    rlib in `fz build`. Decouples the two build paths.

(γ) Add a checksum check at AOT-setup: assert that fz_aot_setup and
    fz_aot_run_main agree on Process's size/layout via a generated
    constant.

(δ) Just document the assumption and rely on `cargo clean` whenever
    runtime/ changes. Trust cargo to do the right thing.

For the immediate kf2 closeout, (δ) is what landed (clean rebuild
fixed it). File this as a defensive ticket against recurrence.
title: fz_runtime staticlib gets stale when fz src and runtime/src diverge in cargo build
